message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Importance</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>A steady increase in acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) cases worldwide is causing some regions of the world to withstand a third or even fourth wave of contagion. Swift detection of SARS-CoV-2 infection is paramount for the containment of cases, prevention of sustained contagion; and most importantly, for the reduction of mortality.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objective</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>To evaluate the performance and validity of the COVISTIX™ rapid antigen test, for the detection of SARS-CoV-2 in an unselected population and compare it to Panbio™ rapid antigen test and RT-PCR.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Design</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>This is comparative effectiveness study; samples were collected at two point-of-care facilities in Mexico City between May and August 2021.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Participants</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Recruited individuals were probable COVID-19 cases, either symptomatic or asymptomatic persons that were at risk of infection due to close contact to SARS-CoV-2 positive cases.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Diagnostic intervention</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>RT-PCR was used as gold standard for detection of SARS-CoV-2 in nasal and nasopharyngeal swabs, study subjects were tested in parallel either with the COVISTIX™ or with Panbio™ rapid antigen test.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Main outcome</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Diagnostic performance of the COVISTIX™ assay is adequate in all commers since its accuracy parameters were not affected in samples collected after 7 days of symptom onset, and it detected almost 65% of samples with a Ct-value between 30 and 34.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>For the population tested with COVISTIX™ (n=783), specificity and sensitivity of the was 96.0% (CI95% 94.0-98.0) and 81% (CI95% 76.0-85.0), as for the Panbio™ (n=2202) population, was 99.0% (CI95%: 0.99-1.00) and 62% (CI%: 58.0-64.0%), respectively.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusions and relevance</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>The COVISTIX™ rapid antigen test shows a high performance in all comers, thus, this test is also adequate for testing patients who have passed the peak of viral shedding or for asymptomatic patients.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Analytical performances of the COVISTIX™ and Panbio™ antigen rapid tests for SARS-CoV-2 detection in an unselected population (all commers)
